action.hypothesis: A modest LR reduction from 8e-4 to 6e-4 may improve the topk K=128 EF=12 signum training trajectory by reducing late-stage noise or overshoot while preserving the current architecture and optimizer.
action.summary: Keep the topk mainline recipe fixed and run a single-variable learning-rate probe at LR=6e-4 against the K=128 EF=12 signum baseline.
action.self_review: This follows the prescribed mainline order after the clean baseline and changes only LR. The prompt does not expose detailed curve-shape metrics, so this is the more conservative LR probe before considering optimizer changes.
notes_to_memory: Round 2 asks one question only: whether a modest LR reduction improves the topk K=128 EF=12 signum curve relative to the clean baseline.
next_hypotheses: If LR=6e-4 improves stability but converges too slowly, test LR=1e-3 as the opposite single-axis LR probe before changing optimizer.
If the LR sweep does not improve the topk mainline, keep LR anchored and test OPTIMIZER=adam as the next single-variable change.

result.description: Keep the topk mainline recipe fixed and run a single-variable learning-rate probe at LR=6e-4 against the K=128 EF=12 signum baseline.
result.self_review: This follows the prescribed mainline order after the clean baseline and changes only LR. The prompt does not expose detailed curve-shape metrics, so this is the more conservative LR probe before considering optimizer changes.
